notes: Cross-session memory for AV Intelligence Digest Agent v5.0. Read at session start (Task 0). Written back at session end (Task 7). Schema v2.0 adds source_failures, company_tracking, consecutive_no_change_days, conflict_age_days.

ongoing_stories.headline: China nationwide AV permit freeze
ongoing_stories.last_status: Active — no resumption timeline confirmed as of June 2, 2026 (Day 34). MIIT suspended all new AV permits following Apollo Go system-wide outage. Freeze blocks fleet additions, new test programmes, and new city expansions. Existing services continue to operate in permitted cities.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Outage trigger date disputed — March 31, 2026 (EVWire/DIGITIMES, localized Wuhan incident, Score: 2) vs April 27, 2026 (Bloomberg/Automotive World, system-wide recurrence, Score: 3). Working conclusion: April 27 system-wide event most likely triggered the April 29 MIIT freeze; March 31 was a precursor. LOW PRIORITY — Human verification recommended against Chinese-language official sources.
status_change_triggers: Official MIIT or Ministry of Transport announcement on resumption or extension
Any operator receiving a new AV permit
Any official timeline given or revised
Any additional operator sanctioned or penalized
Official Baidu statement confirming or clarifying the disputed outage trigger date
Any regulatory document citing a specific confirmed date

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go outage trigger date — March 31 vs April 27 conflict
ongoing_stories.last_status: Unresolved — Baidu has not publicly disclosed root cause or confirmed the triggering date. Two credible source groups cite different dates. No official clarification issued.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: March 31 cited by EVWire/DIGITIMES (Score: 2 — Tier 3, single corroboration, specific date). April 27 cited by Bloomberg/Automotive World (Score: 3 — Tier 3, two corroborations, more recent reporting). Working conclusion: April 27 system-wide event is the more likely MIIT trigger. LOW PRIORITY — Human verification recommended.
status_change_triggers: Official Baidu statement confirming or clarifying the date
Regulatory document from MIIT citing a specific date
Third-party independent investigation finding published by credible source

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo NHTSA recall — 3,791 vehicles, flooding defect
ongoing_stories.last_status: Open — interim OTA fix deployed (tighter weather restrictions, updated map data) but no permanent fix submitted to NHTSA. NHTSA has flagged in writing that no permanent fix has been received. San Antonio and Atlanta operations remain paused.
status_change_triggers: Permanent fix submitted to or approved by NHTSA
Recall scope expanded or narrowed
NHTSA closes or escalates the investigation
Waymo issues a new interim fix or OTA update
San Antonio or Atlanta operations formally resumed

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo freeway ride suspension — construction zone edge cases
ongoing_stories.last_status: Ongoing — surface streets operational in SF, LA, Phoenix, Miami. All freeway rides suspended since May 21, 2026 after viral video of vehicle navigating construction cones. Company integrating technical learnings; expects resumption 'soon' — no date given. Root cause not publicly disclosed.
status_change_triggers: Official freeway resumption announced by Waymo
Suspension extended to additional cities
Root cause publicly disclosed
Software fix confirmed deployed via OTA
Atlanta operations formally resumed

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go Wuhan service shutdown — ongoing post-outage
ongoing_stories.last_status: Status contested as of June 2, 2026. Registry records shutdown since April 1, 2026 pending official investigation. Informal tourism guide (China Survival Kit, unverified, undated) describes Wuhan service as operational 24/7 and characterizes outage as a 12-hour disruption. No official Baidu or MIIT statement confirms resumption. HIGH PRIORITY conflict — human verification recommended.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Registry/Automotive World: service still shut down pending investigation (Score: 4 — Tier 3, multiple corroborations, specific official Apollo Go statement, most recent credible reporting). China Survival Kit tourist guide: service operational 24/7, outage was 12-hour disruption (Score: 0 — unverified source, no date, no corroboration). Working conclusion: service likely still shut down, but informal signals suggest possible quiet resumption. HIGH PRIORITY — recommend direct verification via Apollo Go app, Baidu IR, or Chinese-language news.
status_change_triggers: Official Apollo Go or Baidu announcement of Wuhan service resumption
Regulatory approval granted for Wuhan operations to restart
Root cause of outage publicly disclosed by Baidu
Credible Tier 1-3 source confirming service operational status in Wuhan

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo Ojai — US Senate scrutiny of Zeekr/Geely manufacturing partnership
ongoing_stories.last_status: Active — Senator Bernie Moreno (R-OH) criticized Waymo at Senate Commerce, Science & Transportation Committee hearing for manufacturing partnership with Zeekr (Geely subsidiary). Waymo maintains compliance with forthcoming 2027 connected-vehicle rules. California fare approval for Ojai pending. No legislation introduced.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Senate hearing date unconfirmed in sources reviewed — flagged LOW PRIORITY.
status_change_triggers: Legislation introduced targeting Chinese-manufactured AV vehicles
California PUC issues or denies Ojai commercial fare approval
USTR or Commerce Dept opens formal review of Waymo-Zeekr arrangement
Senate committee issues formal report or subpoena
Waymo changes or terminates Zeekr manufacturing agreement
